title: deschampsia cespitosa var. submutica
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa var. submutica, also known as Tufted Hair Grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe, Asia, and North America, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of colds, sore throats, and coughs. Herbal preparations of Deschampsia cespitosa var. submutica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems of the plant and steeping them in hot water.
